Fnrn Pollrlcnr- PRAcrlcEs CouvtlssloN
-ll8 J Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento. CA 958 l'l-2319
(916) -121-5660 . Far (916) 322-0886
March 16,2005
David L. Herman, DeputY
County of Madera
Otfice of the County Counsel
333 West Olive Avenue
Madera, CA93637
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-05-009
Dear Mr. Hennan
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the County of Madera
fbr aclvice regardirrg the conflict of interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").1
QUESTION
You have asked rvhether school districts and various independent local
government agencies, for rvhich the Madera County Board of Supervisors is the code
revierving body, may act as the filing officer for statements of economic interests (Form
700) filecl by public officials designated in the agencies' conflict of interest codes, or if
not, rvhether the county must be the filing officer.
CONCLUSION
The Boarcl of Supen,isors (the "Board") is the filing oftjcer for statements of
economic interests filed by heads of agencies and members of boards and commissions
located rvholly rvithin the county but not under the direct jurisdiction of the Board. At
the Boarcl's discretion, these otllcials may file statements rvith their agencies, rvhich must
fonvarcl original statements to the Board, or they may file directly rvith the Board. All of
the agencies describecl in your letter appear to be subject to this requirement, although rve
clo not have sufficient facts to determine rvhether all of the officials you have described
ri'ould be considered "heads of agencies."
I Gore*nrent Code sections 81000 - 9101,1. Cornmission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
Re
.t
18109-18997, of the Calitbmia Code of Regulations.


File No. A-05-009
Page No. 2
FACTS
q
you haue asked tvhether the individual agencies listed below may be designated
as the filing officer for statements of economic interests (Form 700) filed by members
and officials of those agencies or, if not, whether the county of Madera must be the filing
officer. your letter refers to these agencies as "school districts and independent districts"
in the County of Madera.
Madera CountY Office of Education
School districts
A cemeterY district
MaderaCountyMosquitoandVectorControlDistrict
Madera Irrigation District
A rvater clistrict organized pursuant to Chapter 13 of the Water Code
Madera County Transportation Commission
Madera County Transportation Authority
Madera County Local Agency Formation Commission
First 5 Madera CountY
Maclera County Economic Development Commission
ANALYSIS
The Act requires that the assets and income of public officials lvhich may be
materially aft'ected by their official actions be fully disclosed, and in appropriate
circumstances, the oitj.iul, should be clisqualified from acting in order that conflicts of
interest may be avoicled. (Section 81002(c).) "Public officials, rvhether elected or
appointed, should perfonn their duties in an irnpartial manner, free from bias caused by
their orvn financial interests or the financial interests of persons rvho have supported
them." (Section 8 1001(b).) In accomplishing these goals, the Act requires public
officials to file on onnuol siatement otteconomic interests. Governmental positions
subject to this filing requirement fall into trvo separate categories' First, persons holding
positions set forth in ,.ition 87200 are "statutory filers" and must disclose all of their
economic interests. Designated employees. inclucling department heads not'listed in
section g7200, must also il" on annual statement of economic interests pursuant to their
agency's contlict of interest code. (Section 87302(b)')
Your question concerns statements of economic interests filed by'designated
employees. Slction 87500 governs rvhere statements of economic interests must be filed'
Section 87500(,) Provides:
..IH]eaclsofagencies,membersofboardsorcommissions
not under a department of state goverrlment or members of
boards or commissions not under the jurisdiction of a local
legislative bociy -- one original rvith the agency, lvhich shall
make and retain a copy and forward the original to the code
.t


.t
File No. A-05-009
Page No. 3
revierving body which shall be the filing officer. In its
discretion, the code reviewing body may provide that the
original be filed directly with the code reviewing body and
that no copy be retained by the agency."
The Madera County Board of Supervisors is the code reviewing body for county
agencies and local gov€rnment agencies with jurisdiction wholly within the county.
(Section 82011(b).) Because the school districts and independent districts that are the
iubject of your request are not under the direct jurisdiction of the Board, section 87500(i)
,.qri..r that the Board of Supervisors be the filing officer for their board and commission
members, as rvell as for managers and supervisors, if they are the head of their respective
districts.2 (Grumrald Advice Letter, No. I-00-183a.) For designated employees who are
not members of boards and commissions and are not deemed to be agency heads, section
87500(o) governs rvhere statements must be filed:
"Persons not mentioned above - one original with the
agency or with the code reviewing body as provided by the
code revierving body in the agency's conflict of interest
code."
Thus, to the extent that any of the designated employees identified in 1'our letter
are not consiclerecl the head of their districts, the individual district may act as filing
officers for those employees. Such a determination must be made by the district and the
code revierving bodY.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(et6) 322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
r\

By:
Carla Wardlorv
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

I A conflict ofinterest code does not establish any disclosure obligation for officials rvho are also
specified in section 87200 if they are designated in the code in that same capacity or if the geographical
jurisdiction of the local governnient agency is the same as or is rvholly included within the jurisdiction in
rvhich the official must ieport his or her financial interests pursuant to section 87200. (Section 87302;
regulation I 8730(bX3).)
